metadata.title: Multimodal Agent Systems for Scientific Paper Review
metadata.subtitle: SNUPI Lab literature review draft

slides.title: Multimodal Agent Systems for Scientific Paper Review
slides.subtitle: SNUPI Lab literature review draft
speaker_notes: Open with the problem: researchers want slide drafts from notes, URLs, or PDFs without hand-formatting every slide.

slides.title: TL;DR
bullets: Normalize every request into a deck spec before generating slides.
Prefer one dominant figure or equation per analytical slide.
Use lightweight extraction first and insert explicit placeholders when confidence is low.
speaker_notes: This is the operating policy for the skill rather than a claim about any one external project.

slides.title: Why A Two-Step Pipeline Works
bullets: Input collection and slide rendering fail for different reasons, so they should be separated.
A normalized slide spec gives the model a stable target and keeps rendering deterministic.
The renderer can enforce lab rules like title size, footer formatting, and one-visual slides.
speaker_notes: Emphasize that the spec is the contract between planning and layout.

slides.title: Reference Architecture For The Skill
bullets: Paper ingestion and slide rendering stay loosely coupled.
The final PPTX remains editable because the layout stage is deterministic.

assets.caption: Example pipeline diagram for a paper-to-slides workflow.

slides.title: Use A Cost-Aware Drafting Objective
bullets: Higher extraction confidence should reduce the number of placeholders.
Longer decks should be penalized when they add low-value detail.
speaker_notes: This slide is illustrative, not quoted from a paper.

assets.caption: Illustrative objective for balancing utility, density, and placeholder count.

slides.title: Takeaways And Open Questions
bullets: Default to autonomous draft generation once the essentials are known.
Keep the parser optional so normal lab machines can still use the skill.
Future work: stronger table extraction and automatic source-to-slide citation placement.
speaker_notes: End by connecting the implementation back to weekly lab workflows.
